campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help local youth pay for recreation programs. They also discussed extending a temporary rule that was put in place urgently, and moved forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian crossing over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue by applying for grant funding.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth recreation scholarship fund
Extension of interim ordinance #2326
Hamilton Avenue bike/pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 - grant application and feasibility study

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to gather community input on a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing from residents, the council decided to move forward with the proposal and skip the usual first reading process so they can vote on it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to get resident feedback
New ordinance (city rule) being fast-tracked for approval
Council waived first reading to speed up the process
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, primarily to approve meeting minutes from various sessions held in March. The council also moved forward with authorizing the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully detailed in this agenda.
keyTopics: Approved multiple sets of March meeting minutes from council sessions
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc. (project details unclear from agenda)
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several agreements and plans affecting the airport and affordable housing. Key decisions included extending the contract with the baggage handling system company at the airport, addressing how affordable housing restrictions work when subsidies end, and planning improvements to the city's storm sewer system.
keyTopics: Airport baggage system contract extension with Vanderlande Industries
Affordable housing protections when subsidy programs expire
Storm sewer system master planning and upgrades
FEMA San José Family Camp project actions

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held a study session to review Mountain View's plans and budget for building and improving city infrastructure over the next year. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in the city.
keyTopics: Next year's capital improvement projects and spending plans
Teen Month proclamation
National Volunteer Week recognition

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their regular meeting on April 7, 2026, where they recognized Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. The council opened with the Pledge of Allegiance and highlighted these two important observances as special orders of business.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
City Council regular meeting procedures

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop to help residents understand and use OpenGov, a tool that shows how the city spends taxpayer money. The council also discussed rules requiring lobbyists to register and report their activities if they try to influence city decisions.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demonstration
City budget spending visibility for residents
Lobbying registration requirements under municipal code
How to track who influences city decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal disclosures about how residents can challenge City decisions. If you disagree with a City decision, you have 90 days from the decision date to file a lawsuit, which is the legal deadline set by state law.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the City over decisions
Legal challenges to City quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6
Important legal rights notification for residents

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several administrative items, including an update to the city's five-year housing and community development plan, requests from council members for new projects to work on, and a proposed change to how city council vacancies are filled. The council also approved meeting minutes and reviewed upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Changes to the council vacancy procedure
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling of future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner wants to upgrade the building's historic status, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of new floor space as a bonus for preserving and improving the historic structure and making it safer against earthquakes.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic status upgrade from Category 4 to Category 2
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Downtown Commercial zone building in pedestrian shopping district
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
Approval of previous meeting minutes from March 12, 2026
